definition: A round fruit that can be red, green, or yellow. Usually sweet or tart.
A warm orange-yellow color, like honey.
A small sign or symbol worn on clothing to show who you are or what group you belong to.
Cooked in an oven using dry heat, like bread or cookies.
A flat area of sand or small stones next to the ocean or a lake.
Strands of hair or rope woven together in a pattern.
A common food made from flour and water, baked in an oven.
A piece of furniture with four legs and a back, made for one person to sit on.
A white or grey mass of tiny water droplets floating in the sky.
To move your body in a rhythm, usually to music.
A large piece of fabric used to cover a window; a curtain.
Images and stories your mind creates while you are asleep.
The planet we live on, the third from the sun.
A wooden or metal barrier built around a yard or field.
The flat surface at the bottom of a room that you walk on.
The sweet, edible part of a plant that contains seeds, like apples or oranges.
A hard, clear material used to make windows and drinking cups.
Feeling good, pleased, or joyful.
A small metal joint that lets a door or lid open and close.
A dome-shaped shelter built from blocks of ice and snow.
A soft, wobbly food made from fruit juice and sugar.
A tool with a sharp blade used for cutting food.
A yellow citrus fruit with a very sour taste.
Energy that allows us to see things. It comes from the sun, lamps, and fire.
A meal eaten in the middle of the day.
A small furry animal with a long tail, big ears, and sharp teeth.
Slightly wet; not completely dry.
Sounds arranged in a pattern of rhythm and melody that people enjoy listening to.
The dark part of the day when the sun has set and the moon is visible.
A type of show where the actors sing their words instead of speaking them.
A thin flat material used for writing, drawing, or printing.
A large musical instrument with black and white keys that you press to make sounds.
Rounded and slightly chubby in a pleasant way.
The sound a duck makes.
A warm bed covering made by sewing together layers of fabric, often in colorful patterns.
A woman who rules a country, or the wife of a king.
A device that receives signals through the air and plays music or voices.
To burn your skin with very hot liquid or steam.
A long reptile with no legs that moves by sliding along the ground.
To clean a floor by pushing dirt with a broom.
A piece of furniture with a flat top and legs, used for eating or working.
A brightly colored flower that grows from a bulb in spring.
Below or beneath something else.
How important or useful something is.
Very bright and clear, easy to see or remember.
A clear liquid that falls as rain and fills oceans, rivers, and lakes. We need it to survive.
The part of your body that connects your hand to your arm.
A large, expensive boat used for sailing or racing.
Not old; having lived for only a short time.
A large African animal that looks like a horse with black and white stripes.
A set of pages bound together with a cover, filled with writing or pictures.
A sweet baked food, often decorated with frosting and eaten at celebrations.
A small, flat, round piece of metal used as money.
A flat panel that opens and closes to let people in or out of a room.
An animal that lives in water, breathes through gills, and has fins to swim.
An activity with rules that people play for fun, either alone or with others.
The place where you live and feel safe and comfortable.
To push off the ground with your feet and move through the air.
A light frame covered in fabric or paper, flown in the wind on a long string.
A large area of water surrounded by land on all sides.
A device that produces light, used to brighten a room.
The large round object that orbits Earth and shines at night.
A small structure that birds build from twigs and grass to hold their eggs.
A large outdoor area with grass and trees where people relax and play.
Water that falls from clouds in drops.
A tiny part of a plant that can grow into a new plant when put in soil.
Frozen water that falls from clouds as soft white flakes.
A ball of burning gas far away in space that looks like a tiny light in the night sky.
A tall plant with a thick wooden trunk, branches, and leaves.
Air that moves from one place to another.
A very small insect that lives in large groups underground.
A small flying mammal that sleeps during the day and hunts at night.
A small furry pet that purrs and meows.
A popular furry pet that barks and wags its tail.
A round or oval object laid by birds, containing a baby animal inside.
A device with spinning blades that moves air to cool you down.
A covering worn on top of your head.
A colored liquid used in pens for writing and drawing.
A sweet spread made by cooking fruit with sugar, put on bread or toast.
A small metal object shaped to open a specific lock.
A thick piece of wood cut from a tree, often used for fires.
A flat drawing that shows where places are, used to find your way.
A material made of threads woven together with holes in between, used for catching things.
A bird with big round eyes that hunts at night and makes a hooting sound.
A tool filled with ink used for writing.
The giant star at the center of our solar system that gives us light and heat.
An object made for children to play with.
A large vehicle used to carry people or goods.
Covered in water or another liquid.
To close something using a zipper, or to move somewhere very fast.
A very deep hole or drop that seems to have no bottom.
A bright, clear blue color, like a cloudless sky.
A musical instrument with a round body and strings, common in country and folk music.
A large, bright, strongly burning fire.
Quick and full of energy.
Firm and dry, making a crunching sound when you bite into it.
In stories, a small magical creature that lives underground and is skilled at mining.
A large, powerful bird of prey with sharp eyes and a curved beak.
A long, narrow inlet of the sea between steep cliffs, common in Norway.
A small, brief flash of light.
A symbol or picture used as writing, like the ones ancient Egyptians carved in stone.
A light brownish-green color, often used to describe eye color.
A creamy white color, or the hard white material that elephant tusks are made of.
Happy, cheerful, and full of good humor.
A person chosen to be part of a jury in a court of law.
A small booth or stand where things like tickets or newspapers are sold.
A natural talent or special skill for doing something.
A large black bird, similar to a crow, known for being very intelligent.
A creature from ancient myths with a human head and a lion's body.
A small herb with tiny leaves used to add flavor to food.
Going further than what is normal or expected; extreme.
A smooth, graceful ballroom dance performed in three-beat rhythm.
To officially force someone to leave a place, often as a punishment.
A small rocking bed for a baby.
To make something slightly wet, or to reduce someone's excitement.
To start a journey or begin something new.
The front of a building, or a false appearance that hides the truth.
To shine with a sparkling, wet light.
A barrier to jump over in a race, or any problem that gets in your way.
A puzzle made of many oddly shaped pieces that fit together to make a picture.
A container used to boil water, with a handle and a spout.
A large open field covered with grass and wildflowers.
Someone who is new to an activity and is still learning.
In ancient times, a person believed to receive messages from the gods about the future.
To satisfy your thirst by drinking.
A clever question or puzzle with a tricky answer.
Not very often; rarely.
A cold, flat landscape with no trees, found in the Arctic where the ground is often frozen.
To give someone helpful suggestions about what to do.
To confuse someone completely so they cannot understand something.
A deep valley with steep rocky walls, often with a river at the bottom.
To leave a place, especially to start a journey.
The energy and hard work you put into doing something.
Soft, calm, and careful; not rough or harsh.
Empty inside; having a hole or empty space within.
To set something on fire, or to cause something to start.
A thick, dense tropical forest full of plants and wild animals.
To stay somewhere longer than expected, as if you do not want to leave.
Related to the sea or ocean.
A sweet liquid found inside flowers that bees collect to make honey.
To beat someone by being cleverer than them.
Calm and peaceful; not easily upset or excited.
Attractively old-fashioned or unusual in a charming way.
To start something again after stopping for a while.
A group of performers such as actors, dancers, or musicians who work together.
To leave someone or something behind and not return.
Under something; lower than something else.
So impressed or amazed by something that you can barely think straight.
When the moon passes in front of the sun and blocks its light.
Real and true; not fake or pretend.
When things work well together, or when musical notes sound good together.
Extremely large or great in size.
Fair treatment and the right outcome, especially by the law.
The room in a home where food is cooked and prepared.
Free time when you are not working and can do things you enjoy.
Something that is difficult to explain or understand.
Not taking sides in a disagreement; not supporting either option.
A piece of land where fruit trees are grown.
One of the first people to explore or settle in a new area.
How good something is; a high standard.
Not joking; thinking carefully about something important.
A great success or victory after a lot of effort.
To move forward, or to make progress.
Being steady and not falling over, or having equal amounts of things.
A person whose job is to construct buildings and other structures.
Made up of many different parts and difficult to understand.
The system of money, trade, and business in a country.
The ability to do what you want without being controlled by others.
A room or building where works of art are displayed for people to see.
The line far away where the sky appears to meet the land or sea.
The act of asking questions to find out information.
A long trip from one place to another.
A country or area ruled by a king or queen.
The freedom to live and act the way you choose.
To make something look bigger than it really is, like with a magnifying glass.
A connected system of people or things that share information or resources.
To watch something carefully and pay close attention to it.
A serious statement that you will definitely do something.
Treating someone or something with care and consideration because you value them.
A place that protects you from bad weather or danger.
A building where plays or movies are performed for an audience.
A vehicle with two wheels that you sit on and pedal with your feet.
A passage of water connecting two larger bodies of water, or a TV station.
To take something to a person or place.
A basic part that something is made of.
The management of money, savings, and investments.
A huge, slow-moving river of ice found in mountains or polar regions.
To find out the size, length, or amount of something.
The way a person thinks about life and the future.
A line that marks the edge of an area or shows where one thing ends and another begins.
A chart that shows all the days, weeks, and months of the year.
A female child in relation to her parents.
To carefully think about something and decide how good or useful it is.
Information or opinions given to someone to help them improve.
Images, drawings, or visual designs, especially on a computer or in print.
A large building where sick or injured people are treated by doctors and nurses.
To recognize and name what something or someone is.
A system of words and rules used by people to communicate with each other.
A very large, tall area of land that rises high above the surrounding ground.
To plan and follow a route to get from one place to another.
A special event or a particular time when something happens.
Belonging to or concerning one particular person, not shared with others.
An amount or number of something.
A person who is part of your family.
A careful plan made to achieve a goal.
A folding device held over your head to keep you dry in the rain.
Something that can change or be changed.
Animals and plants that live in nature, not kept by humans.
An exciting or unusual experience, often involving some risk.
Very pleasing to look at or experience.
To do something enjoyable to mark a special occasion.
The path or course along which something or someone moves.
The tools, machines, or gear needed for a particular activity.
The way something is shaped or arranged, or the process of creating it.
A rule or piece of advice that helps you decide how to do something.
Mattering a great deal; having a big effect.
Information and understanding gained through learning or experience.
Everything you can see when you look across an area of land.
A medical procedure where a doctor cuts into the body to fix a problem.
The ability to become something great in the future.
Something you ask when you want to find out information.
The process of getting better after being sick or going through a hard time.
Your name written in your own personal style, used to sign documents.
To move people or things from one place to another.
To know the meaning of something or to make sense of it.
Extremely good or impressive; causing feelings of joy.
Friendly, kind, and generous toward other people.
Having a strong desire to do something and not giving up.
So good that it can be used as a model for others to follow.
So large, powerful, or impressive that it causes respect or fear.
A person who studies the features of the earth, including land, oceans, and climate.
Done without any plan or order; randomly.
Famous and respected because of past achievements.
Two things placed side by side so you can compare them.
A person who cannot stop themselves from stealing things, even if they do not need them.
So complicated or twisted that it is very hard to find your way through.
A conversation between people trying to reach an agreement.
So large or strong that it is very difficult to deal with.
A person who thinks deeply about big questions like the meaning of life.
The most perfect or typical example of something.
To spread information widely so that many people can receive it.
Very confident, colorful, and attention-grabbing in style or behavior.
To see or hear something that is not really there.
A tube with mirrors and colored pieces that create beautiful changing patterns when you look through it.
To change completely from one form into another, like a caterpillar becoming a butterfly.
A doctor who specializes in conditions affecting the brain and nervous system.
At exactly a 90-degree angle to something; completely upright or straight across.
